summary:
The speed at which an inhalation anesthesia induces effects primarily depends on its physiochemical characteristics such as gas-lipid partition coefficient; drugs with low values induce quicker due to better absorption into tissues before reaching effective levels in brain tissue. Neonates metabolize these substances rapidly enhancing efficacy but do so without altering overall principles significantly affecting general trends observed clinically across different age groups or patient populations. To summarize concisely while maintaining focus:

Inhalational agents' rate of onset mainly hinges upon their chemical composition influencing how swiftly they diffuse through bodily barriers versus physiological variables having secondary impacts generally preserving overarching patterns seen among various patients regardless of specific conditions present during administration procedures performed by clinicians aiming towards optimal outcomes
